The nexus binary at `/Users/jeremy.pollock/node_modules/.bin/nexus` is a symlink to `/Users/jeremy.pollock/node_modules/@local-labs-jpollock/local-addon-nexus-ai/bin/nexus.js`. This is the user-level (not global) npm installation. The `nexus update` command updated the global npm package but not this user-level one.

The issue is that:
1. The nexus CLI is installed locally at `~/node_modules/` (not system-wide)
2. `nexus update` runs `npm install -g` which installs to global npm location, but the PATH picks up the local `~/node_modules/.bin/` version first


3. So the old v0.2.1 is still being used

But the real blocker is that the MCP server isn't responding. The CLI times out trying to connect to Local, and commands like `sites list` and `audit plugins` are likely routing through the IPC/MCP channel which is down. The GraphQL server on port 4000 is working though, so I should focus on what commands can actually run right now.
